scores.head_floor_note: Engine's sandbox --disable all on HEAD reports 5 — but this is misleading. Engine's strip removes __asm__ blocks + the `asm("v1")` qualifier from the register pin, but does NOT remove the dead `u8 *new_var; ...(new_var = &D_800900EC)` alias coercion. So the 5 is still measured against C that includes the new_var cheat-by-spelling. HEAD's HONEST pure-C floor (without ANY cheats) is 6 == this candidate.

sessions.lever: inline D_800A3844 = arr[arg0]; drop pin + dead-local alias

reviewer.evidence: not invoked — candidate is honest pure-C, no cheats; not committed (oracle would break)

remaining_gap.summary: Pure scheduling — GCC hoists the array load 3 positions earlier than target. Target has D_80102780/D_80102781 stores, then array load, then D_800A3894 stores. Mine has array load first, then D_80102780/D_80102781 stores. All registers match target ($v0=0xFF, $v1=1, $a0=arg0_new); 47/47 insns; just 6 position-swap diffs.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: None — HEAD uses cheat-asm (register-asm pin + dead-local alias `new_var = &D_800900EC`) to anchor scheduling. Both are forbidden by [[inline-asm-policy]] / [[no-new-park-categories]].

next_hypotheses: Find a pure-C lever that anchors the array load to its source position without cheats. The load has INSN_PRIORITY ≥ stores (load → sw chain depth 2 vs store depth 1), so GCC's scheduler hoists it.
Try directed permuter from candidate.c base — randomization may find a structural restructure (e.g. compute address into intermediate const pointer that isn't combine-folded).
Try declaring a const aliasing variable for &D_800900EC at file scope — if it survives combine, may create a serialization barrier.

Instrument cc1's sched.c (BB2_SCHED_DEBUG hook from tmp/gccdbg) to confirm the load's INSN_PRIORITY beats the stores' — then look for any C structural change that lowers the load's depth or raises the stores' depth.

rejected_forms.form: explicit `arg0 = ((s32*)&D_800900EC)[arg0]` reassignment, source order matches target
rejected_forms.reason: Creates an extra long-lived pseudo for arg0_new; competes with constant 1 for $v1, pushing constant 1 to $a1 (3 reg-rename diffs on top of the same scheduling hoist).

rejected_forms.form: `func_8003AE5C(arg0)` instead of `func_8003AE5C(D_800A3844)` (use C local instead of memory reload)
rejected_forms.reason: Makes arg0 live across file_LoadOverlay call; forces callee-save reservation. Target reloads from D_800A3844 to fill the jal delay slot (per [[store-before-jal]] pattern).
Cheat-asm — register-asm pin per [[inline-asm-policy]]. Not a committable form.

rejected_forms.score: n/a — part of the cheat cluster
rejected_forms.reason: Dead-local-alias coercion per [[inline-asm-injection]] / [[no-new-park-categories]] cheats-by-spelling. The variable has no semantic purpose; its only effect is to anchor scheduling. Not committable.
